# Artifact Registry â€” Schema and Usage

The artifact registry (`.github/artifacts/REGISTRY.md`) is an **agent-maintained, append-only** record of significant outputs produced by agent workflows in this repository. It provides a bird's-eye view of all completed work across the repository's lifetime.

## Purpose

- Answer "What has been produced in this repo and when?"
- Enable cross-referencing between JIRA tickets, git branches, and their PR outcomes
- Provide a risk history (aggregate view of past risk levels)
- Support project handoffs and onboarding

## Registry File

`.github/artifacts/REGISTRY.md` â€” created and maintained by agents via the `register-artifact` skill.

> **This file is NOT generated by the assembler.** The assembler writes only this schema file so that re-running the assembler never overwrites accumulated agent history.

The registry is initialized the first time a workflow reaches `Ready` phase and then appended to on every subsequent completed workflow.

## Entry Format

Each completed workflow appends exactly one row to the registry table:

```markdown
| Timestamp | Type | Ticket | PR | Branch | URL | Actor | Risk | Summary |
|-----------|------|--------|----|--------|-----|-------|------|---------|
| 2026-05-07T14:00:00Z | feat | PROJ-123 | #42 | feat/proj-123-login | https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42 | pr-author | LOW | Added OAuth2 login flow; 3 tests added |
```

## Field Definitions

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `Timestamp` | ISO-8601 | UTC datetime when the workflow reached `Ready` |
| `Type` | enum | `feat` Â· `fix` Â· `chore` Â· `refactor` Â· `docs` Â· `test` â€” matches branch prefix |
| `Ticket` | string | JIRA ticket key (e.g., `PROJ-123`) |
| `PR` | string | GitHub PR number with `#` prefix (e.g., `#42`) |
| `Branch` | string | Git branch name |
| `URL` | string | Direct URL to the merged/ready PR |
| `Actor` | string | Agent that finalized the PR (always `pr-author`) |
| `Risk` | enum | `LOW` Â· `MEDIUM` Â· `HIGH` Â· `CRITICAL` â€” from review phase |
| `Summary` | string | One-sentence human-readable description of what was done |

## Mutability Rules

- The **table header row** is IMMUTABLE â€” never change column definitions
- All **data rows** are APPEND-ONLY â€” once written, a row may never be edited or deleted
- If a workflow is abandoned (PR closed without merging), no row is appended
- If an error in a row is discovered after writing, annotate the next row's Summary with a correction note rather than editing the existing row

## Use the `register-artifact` skill

All registry operations (initialization and appending) are handled via the `register-artifact` skill.
